Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use S3 Inventory, Storage Lens, analytics, and sampled reconciliation for object estate, encryption, replication, lifecycle, ownership, and cost evidence.

For S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Defend the Inventory Storage Lens model · Theory Inventory Storage L…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, file, object, policy, transfer, integrity, cache, fault, cost, restore, and controlled AWS transfer evidence: A live ListObjects scan is always cheaper and more complete than inventory reports. Type annotations, generated SDK shapes, and resolved promises do not strengthen that runtime claim.
